channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: United Airlines plane tail clipped while taxiing at O'Hare
item.description: "It felt like we hit a pothole. Some people felt it, some didn't. Nonetheless, a few minutes later, the pilot got on the PA and said that we had tail strike," Lewis said.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames Newsom for California's raging wildfires
item.description: The wildfires that started on Tuesday have killed two people and destroyed more than 1,000 structures.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KREX 5

item.title: Ex-police officer and Proud Boys member gets 14 months in prison for joining Capitol attack
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former police officer who stormed the U.S. Capitol with fellow members of the far-right Proud Boys extremist group was sentenced on Wednesday to 14 months in prison for joining the Jan. 6, 2021, attack by a mob of Donald Trump supporters, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump asks the Supreme Court to block sentencing in his hush money case in New York
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ski patrollers reach deal to end strike at Utah's Park City Mountain Resort
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — Ski patrollers at the biggest U.S. ski resort have reached a deal with Utah's Park City Mountain Resort to end a strike that put a wrench in operations during the busy holiday season and into the new year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US will announce $500M in weapons to be sent to Ukraine
item.description: RAMSTEIN AIR BASE, Germany (AP) — The U.S. is set to provide Ukraine an additional $500 million in weapons quickly pulled from its existing stockpiles as the Biden administration works to get Kyiv in a stronger negotiating position before President-elect Donald Trump takes office, two U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 4 fraternity members at San Diego State University charged after one was set on fire during a 'skit': DA
item.description: The incident occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university over issues of hazing and conduct that threatens the health and safety of students.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about wildfires raining embers onto the Los Angeles area
item.description: Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, with fast-moving flames burning through homes and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Is RadioShack back? Brand attempts return to relevance with consumer products
item.description: Under new leadership, RadioShack is attempting to rebuild its reputation by selling consumer electronics.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Palmer, Fox 5

item.title: Southern US shivers as new winter storm threatens snow for Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — A developing winter storm threatens to drop snow, sleet and freezing rain on parts of Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas as frigid air that escaped the Arctic plunges temperatures to subfreezing levels in some of the southernmost points of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wildfires latest: 2 people killed and over 1,000 structures destroyed, per LA fire chief
item.description: Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, fueled by powerful Santa Ana winds, sending residents fleeing from burning homes through flames, ferocious winds and towering clouds of smoke.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Salt Lake City will be one of the most competitive housing markets in 2025, Zillow report predicts
item.description: The hottest housing markets this year are predicted to be on the East Coast, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, ABC 4

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: President Joe Biden said early Wednesday that he thinks he would have beaten President-elect Donald Trump if he stuck with his reelection bid in an interview with USA Today.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California wildfires force thousands to evacuate and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Mailman's family breaks silence after father of 2 killed on lunch break
item.description: Manhattan 24-year-old Jaia Cruz charged with murder in the stabbing death of U.S. Postal worker Ray Hodge III after a fight in the line at a Harlem deli.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rimmed to the south by a 3-mile stretch of Pacific beach, and to the north by craggy bluffs and deep, verdant canyons, the seaside enclave of Pacific Palisades has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown L
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rare ski patroller strike at largest resort in US causes long lines and closes terrain
item.description: Ski patrollers miffed by wages they say are too low for high living costs have put a wrench in operations at the biggest U.S. ski resort with a rare strike that began over the busy holidays and carried on into the new year's fresh powder.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans is a city marked by tragedy. But don’t call locals resilient
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — She ran around in silver sparkling shoes, her faux chainmail tunic shimmering in the freezing breeze, maneuvering horses made of paper mâché, a giant green dragon, and sheep constructed from milk cartons.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, SHARON LURYE, CLAIRE GALOFARO and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As MLK holiday nears, Bernice King urges people to really focus on his teachings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — As the holiday honoring slain civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. approaches, his daughter is urging people to really focus on his teachings and work throughout the coming year, rather than just quoting him or celebrating for a day. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video: Michigan troopers jump over median to avoid semi barreling through crash scene
item.description: Troopers were investigating a multicar crash that had blocked two left lanes when a tractor-trailer approached.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Man charged with sexual abuse after incident in New York Walmart bathroom
item.description: She was eventually able to push the man back far enough to get out of the bathroom stall and report the incident.
item.dc:creator: Clare Normoyle, NewsNation

item.title: In Cincinnati, roller rink provides warmth and shelter from nation's Arctic blast
item.description: CINCINNATI (AP) — Tirrdell Byrd sat on the floor strumming his guitar as people milled about Over-The-Rhine Recreation Center, winter coats flung over folding chairs, belongings packed into shopping bags and wheeled totes, the smell of pizza lingering.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Girl Scouts getting rid of 2 cookie offerings
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday. The organization revealed the news in a press release issued that morning.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Car abandoned on Colorado ski run; Driver blames GPS
item.description: A driver got a prime (but illegal) parking spot on the slopes Sunday night when their GPS led them right onto a ski run
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, KRON4

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star choked, punched woman before Myrtle Beach arrest, report says
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Winter weather can disrupt. Here's what to do if your flight is canceled
item.description: Winter weather often causes flight delays and cancellations. While airlines can’t control the weather, they are required in the U.S. to provide refunds to customers whose flights are canceled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of animals killed in Dallas shopping center fire
item.description: A fire that broke out at a shopping center in Dallas on Friday morning killed more than 500 animals, most of which were small birds, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5
